summary.largest_items: PP non-woven H563S, LLDPE Film L1220F, PVC S65

bullets: Sabic copo settled $1,200 CIF, with deals heard below $1,200 ex-tax.
NSRP 114G down to 33,400–33,600; Sibur yarn/inj cut 1,300–1,600.
Korean off-grade copo crashed to 30,500–31,500 VND/kg.
Lotte Titan cut LDPE again — 260GG to $1,500 CIF.
Chinese LLDPE hit a market-low 31,500 VND/kg.
Sibur cut Hai Phong PE 400–700 VND/kg.
Formosa's July offer landed at $780 CIF, not the $810–820 first assumed.
Sinopec oil-based to $720 — below the $750–800 production cost.
Coal-based SG-5 broke to 21,600; oil-based to 22,500.

editorial.changedNote: 17 of 50 moved down, led by PP copo & PE

summary: Asian PE fell again — SEA the sharpest, with HDPE −20, LLDPE/C6 −20 and LDPE −20 USD/ton on weak rain-hit demand and rising Korean supply.
Sibur cut Hai Phong offers 400–700 VND/kg; domestic LLDPE Film to 34,200 and HDPE yarn to 35,600 VND/kg incl. VAT.
Sellers entered a price-cutting race: Chinese LLDPE Film traded as low as 31,500 VND/kg; Korean HDPE Film deals closed at 1,150–1,180 USD/ton CIF, far under offers.
paras: According to Platts pricing, in Northeast Asia, the price level of HDPE Film/ yarn/ injection/ blow this week also decreased by 10 USD/ton compared to last week, LLDPE and LDPE decreased by 15 USD/ton. In Southeast Asia, HDPE Film/ yarn/ injection/ blow this week decreased by 20 USD/ton, fluctuating at 1290-1300 USD/ton/CFR; LLDPE decreased by 20 USD/ton down to 1250 USD/ton/CFR, LLDPE metallocene C6 decreased by 20 USD/ton down to 1400 USD/ton/CFR; LDPE also decreased by 20 USD/ton down to 1500 USD/ton/CFR.

Thus, Asian PE prices this week continued to decline, with Southeast Asia decreasing the most sharply. The reason is due to weak demand caused by prolonged rain, reducing buyer attention to imported goods. Meanwhile, the supply is still improving, especially from South Korean suppliers offering more back to Southeast Asia. The short-term outlook for PE remains quite gloomy, with many capabilities still promoting weak and needing additional competitive pressure.

Today, June 11, US-Iran tensions escalated further after Iran declared it would completely freeze Strait of Hormuz operations. This move affected market sentiment. Even so, many market participants assess that, this development may not yet have an immediate impact on short-term PE trends.

Another notable piece of information is that Malaysia's PRefChem is planning to restart its PE plant operations in the latter half of June 2026 after more than 3 months of suspended operations due to an accident at a commercial partner. These two HDPE capacity 400,000 tons/year and LLDPE capacity 350,000 tons/year of Prefchem are expected to restart about a week after the large cracking complex resumes operations.

In the Vietnamese market, today, a distributor offered import prices for LLDPE Film 222WT from Sabic's joint venture plant in China, Sabic Tianjin, at a slight increase of 10 USD/ton compared to last week (last week's offer price dropped sharply by up to 50 USD/ton), the offer price is 1230 USD/ton/CIF Hai Phong, inclusive of CIC charges. Even so, this new offer price has not yet received much response from buyers.

A distributor this week offered South Korea Lotte Hyundai's LDPE Film LF512 to Vietnam at 1470 USD/ton/CIF; offered Lotte Hyundai's HDPE Film FL7000 and HDPE yarn MF5000 both at 1270 USD/ton/CIF. Even so, there is news that a buyer has finalized the import of Lotte Hyundai's HDPE Film goods for only 1180 USD/ton/CIF, up to 90 USD/ton lower than the announced offer price.

Similarly, a distributor offered South Korea SK Chemical's HDPE Film and HDPE yarn to Vietnam at 1200 USD/ton/CIF. However, a source said, it was recorded that there were finalized imports of SK's HDPE Film for only 1150-1170 USD/ton/CIF/ depending on volume and buyer.

Previously yesterday, Lotte Titan Malaysia re-offered July PE goods to Vietnam. The offer prices for LDPE grades decreased by 40 USD/ton compared to last week, bringing grade 260GG down to 1500 USD/ton/CIF; separately, LDPE injection 300YY decreased by 60 USD/ton down to 1560 USD/ton/CIF. The distributor also offered additional HDPE blow and Film goods to Vietnam, the offer price for HDPE Film is at 1280 USD/ton/CIF, HDPE blow at 1330 USD/ton/CIF...

Domestically, Russia's Sibur this week offered LLDPE Film goods to Vietnam at 34,200 VND/kg/ with VAT – down 400 VND/kg compared to last week; offered various HDPE Film grades at 36,500 VND/kg/ with VAT – down 500 VND/kg compared to last week; offered HDPE yarn at 35,600 VND/kg/ with VAT – down 700 VND/kg compared to last week, HDPE injection at 35,200-35,500 VND/kg/ with VAT – down 500 VND/kg compared to last week.

Long Son Petrochemicals (LSP) has sold out HDPE Film inventory after almost 3 weeks of closing the plant, currently LSP only has a small amount of HDPE blow inventory to offer for sale and a lot of LLDPE Film. This week, LSP continues to keep its PE offer prices unchanged (the offer price has moved sideways for the 4th consecutive week); but in actual trading, LSP's distributors are selling at much lower prices.
In the commercial market, mid-week today, many sellers have participated in the "price cutting race" to quickly clear inventory, not wanting to hold goods for too long.

In the North, Lotte Titan's LDPE 260GG goods have been recorded with some sellers lowering the offer price down to 49,000 VND/kg/ with VAT; Chinese Middle Eastern LDPE Film items like Sabic, Borouge were commonly offered down to 48,000 VND/kg/ with VAT.

Saudi Arabia's Qamar's HDPE Film F1 recorded a seller finalizing the sale at 36,500 VND/kg/ excluding VAT, while some other commercial sellers accepted finalized sales at 38,000-38,300 VND/kg/ with VAT, down 1,500-2,000 VND/kg compared to early May.

Saudi Arabia's Sabic's LLDPE Film 218WJ was recorded with many sellers offering at 35,500 VND/kg/ with VAT, equal to the lowest finalized price last week (last week's offer price was commonly in the range of 36,000-37,000 VND/kg/ with VAT depending on the distributor). Notably, Chinese-origin LLDPE Film goods recorded the lowest transaction price at 31,500 VND/kg/ with VAT...

summary: SEA PP slid hardest in Asia — yarn/inj −40 to 1,200, BOPP −50 to 1,220, block copo −80 to 1,210 USD/ton; Vietnam the lowest in the region.
Sabic copo settled at 1,200 USD/ton CIF and word of deals below 1,200 (ex-tax); Sibur cut yarn/inj 1,300–1,600 and copo 700 VND/kg at Hai Phong.
NSRP 114G at 33,400–33,600 and Korean off-grade copo down to 30,500–31,500 VND/kg incl. VAT pulled the domestic market lower.
paras: According to Platts pricing announced today, June 11, in Northeast Asia, the price level of PP yarn/injection/block copo this week kept stable with BOPP and IPP down by 10 USD/ton compared to last week. In Southeast Asia, the price level of PP yarn/injection this week dropped sharply by 40 USD/ton down to 1200 USD/ton/CFR, BOPP dropped by 50 USD/ton down to 1220 USD/ton/CFR, IPP Film dropped by 20 USD/ton down to 1270 USD/ton/CFR, especially PP block copo dropped sharply by up to 80 USD/ton down to 1210 USD/ton/CFR (in Vietnam, these PP types all decreased simultaneously by 30-40 USD/ton and are the lowest in the region).

Thus, the Asian PP market this week continued to move sideways to decrease, with Southeast Asia witnessing the sharpest decline. Due to weaker-than-expected PP supply in China (plants are still in maintenance season), Chinese domestic PP prices mainly held sideways; the FOB export price of Chinese PP remained at a high level (PP yarn/injection around 1220 USD/ton/FOB) but some suppliers still offered to Southeast Asia at much lower prices, because Chinese goods are no longer attractive to Vietnamese buyers. There are even news that some Chinese trading companies started importing PP from Vietnam with goods shipped directly compared to their domestic selling offer prices.

Returning to the Vietnamese market, the offer and finalized prices of Saudi Arabia's Sabic's PP this week also had a high level of competitiveness, even PP copo goods of Sabic were much more outstanding than Chinese, South Korean goods. Late last week, Sabic's PP homo offer price delivered from Singapore to Vietnam was announced at 1200 USD/ton/CIF, PP copo offer price at 1230 USD/ton/CIF. By mid-week today, Sabic's PP copo finalized selling price for shipments from Saudi Arabia to Vietnam was announced at 1200 USD/ton/CIF. Today, there is news that the finalized PP copo price has dropped to below 1200 USD/ton/CIF, excluding 3% import tax.

Domestically, through sources today, Long Son Petrochemicals (LSP), after almost 3 weeks of closing the plant, now only has one ton of PP copo inventory (net PP homo inventory), the offer price this week continues to be kept sideways (still 4 consecutive weeks of price adjustment) but the finalized selling price from the distributor is much lower.

Although LSP paused production, some sources said, domestically in Vietnam, the PP homo market is abundant enough, the production volume from domestic plants is still abundant. The Chinese supply is gradually losing its price competitiveness, but all PP import goods from South Korea are gradually returning to the scene, causing intense competition. Large suppliers from Vietnam are actively speeding up PP exports to the Southeast Asian region and China.

In the market, Russia's Sibur distributor offered PP yarn/injection, non-woven items to Vietnamese buyers at 33,900-34,300 VND/kg/ with VAT/ at Hai Phong port, a sharp drop of 1,300-1,600 VND/kg compared to last week; offered PP copo at 34,800 VND/kg/ with VAT/ at Hai Phong port, down 700 VND/kg compared to last week.

The selling offer price of NSRP's PP 114G in the northern market is commonly around 33,400-33,600 VND/kg/ with VAT, with customers having to record a large volume according to the container value. BSR's PP T3034 goods sold to the South or the North are both lowered to 35,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, yet goods to the North take a much lower price due to high volume compared to NSRP's goods.

Regarding PP copo and random, in the North, a distributor offered South Korea Lotte's PP random copo J560s at 37,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, a sharp drop of up to 8,000 VND/kg compared to the distributor's own offer price in the middle of May. The offer price dropped deeply within the framework of competition, and also because South Korea's off-grade (OG) PP copo goods have dropped to the level of 30,500-31,500 VND/kg/ with VAT.

In the South, the PP copo selling offer price (including all types of goods) is commonly fluctuating in the range of 36,000-38,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, down sharply compared to last week. South Korea Lotte's PP coating M9600 goods last week's cheapest offer was 48,000 VND/kg/ with VAT but this week's price dropped significantly, the lowest finalized price recorded was at 46,000 VND/kg/ with VAT. South Korea Hyosung Vina's PP random goods offered to Vietnam are in the range of about 46,000-47,000 VND/kg/ with VAT...

summary: Asian PVC dropped ~90 USD/ton (China) and 40 (SEA) to 760 USD/ton CFR as Formosa's July offer fell 115–120 USD/ton month-on-month.
Formosa's July offer to Vietnam came in at 780 USD/ton CIF (5% tax); Sinopec oil-based S-1000 offered at 720 CIF Hai Phong — below the 750–800 production cost.
Coal-based SG-5 (Erdos) broke to 21,600 VND/kg incl. VAT (≈21,000 in volume); oil-based Chinese PVC down to 22,500 VND/kg.
paras: According to Platts pricing, the PVC price level in China this week dropped sharply by up to 90 USD/ton compared to last week, down to 760 USD/ton/CFR; the PVC price level in Southeast Asia decreased by 40 USD/ton down to 760 USD/ton/CFR.

Asian PVC prices decreased this week due to weak demand and July offer prices from major suppliers coming in lower, especially Formosa Taiwan's July PVC offer price to the region dropped by 115-120 USD/ton compared to last month. Some customers are waiting for new quotes from other manufacturers, the market sentiment is still quite cautious. Moreover, shipping lines from China increased charges last month to a very high level, causing PVC export activity to slow down.

For the Vietnamese market, some customers have received Formosa Taiwan's July PVC shipment offer prices at 780 USD/ton/CIF Hai Phong, subject to 5% import tax. Even so, the interest is not much. Only a few refineries or chemical processing plants or customers intending to purchase petrochemical goods or services of FPC export shipments are currently interested in signing PVC goods from FPC.

A major distributor offered PVC S-1000 (oil-based) goods from China's Sinopec to Vietnam at 720 USD/ton/CIF Hai Phong, offered 735 USD/ton/CIF Ho Chi Minh City, inclusive of CIC. This price level dropped sharply by about 100-120 USD/ton compared to the middle of May. According to a market source, the total cost to produce oil-based PVC in China at this time is about 750-800 USD/ton. This means that compared to the above-mentioned offer price, the manufacturer must accept selling at a loss and is in a very difficult situation to deeply reduce losses.

It is expected that in the middle of this week, leading Southeast Asian PVC manufacturer AGC Group will offer July PVC shipments to Vietnam. According to sources at the beginning of the week, AGC's offer price will probably also decrease from Formosa's offer price.

Also in domestic trading, mid-week today, a major distributor is currently holding about 500 tons of PVC SG-5 (coal-based) goods originating from China's Erdos, offered for sale at 21,600 VND/kg/ with VAT, 400 VND/kg lower than the lowest selling offer price last week. There was even some clarity that, if bought in large volume, it could be assessed to drop to about 21,000 VND/kg/ with VAT. However, more than 2 weeks after the price arrived this week, the "floor" of coal-based PVC will continue to be broken down even lower.

For oil-based PVC goods, the price reduction is even more pronounced. Some sellers have lowered the offer price of Chinese-origin oil-based PVC down to 22,500 VND/kg/ with VAT, a price gap compared to the most narrowly collected coal-based PVC at the beginning of March 2026. Consequently, many oil-based PVC items of other origins also continue to be under strong downward price pressure, most have traded below 33,500-35 VND/kg/ with VAT...
